captures.notes: Canonical Codrops article for attribution and technical framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Local generated rebuild screenshot showing 3D text volume, pointer influence rings, displaced fragments, and particle traces; not copied from the Codrops demo.
Local generated motion clip isolates fragments settling from a pointer-displaced state back toward readable text; not copied from the Codrops demo.
Standalone local HTML/SVG exercise that reconstructs the primitive with text fragments, pointer field rings, glow layers, and CSS-only drift animation.
Manifest lists only generated local learning evidence plus source-link attribution boundaries.

payload.destruction_state: letters remain readable while fragments are displaced by a pointer field
payload.reconstruction_state: fragments spring back toward a legible word structure
payload.rebuild_scope: local generated typography study from the visible primitive, not a capture of upstream geometry
payload.text_volume_model: bold word face, chromatic offset shadows, and block fragments imply depth without copying TextGeometry

modules.review_notes: Complete for first-pass learning: still image, motion clip, and HTML note all represent the same typography destruction primitive.

payload.field_behavior: particles radiate from the pointer zone and retain faint trace lines to expose force direction
payload.shape_units: small cyan, magenta, yellow, and white points mixed with rectangular text fragments
payload.visual_role: makes the interaction field visible rather than treating destruction as a pure opacity effect

modules.review_notes: Particle vector layer is explicit enough to teach force field, fragment scatter, and readable-state recovery separately.

payload.background_system: dark grid plus pointer rings to locate the destruction field
payload.bloom_proxy: blurred cyan/magenta/yellow accents and bright fragments approximate emissive post-processing
payload.source_boundary: does not claim Codrops shader code, TSL nodes, or exact postprocessing implementation

modules.review_notes: Material notes are complete for visual reconstruction; exact WebGPU shader implementation is intentionally not claimed.

payload.input_model: pointer influence point displaces nearby fragments outward

modules.review_notes: Interaction is represented as named states plus still and motion evidence.

payload.evidence_layer: local PNG proves displaced state, local GIF proves reconstruction motion, HTML note proves the rebuild recipe
payload.review_gap: canonical source remains link-only; local rebuild teaches mechanics without upstream media
payload.route_decision: use readable text for stable claims, displaced fragments for uncertainty, and spring-back motion for repaired evidence state
payload.source_trail: canonical article plus generated local reconstruction
payload.uncertainty: exact upstream WebGPU/TSL implementation is not claimed

modules.review_notes: VULCA mapping is complete because source, evidence, review gap, route decision, and uncertainty are all represented.

summary: Contemporary 3D text destruction reference rebuilt as a local typography and particle-field learning primitive with still, motion, code, and asset evidence.
title: Interactive Text Destruction with Three.js, WebGPU and TSL
why_relevant: Maps directly to technical evidence labels exploding into fragments, showing uncertainty, then rebuilding into a readable claim state.
